plugins.name: commit-craft
plugins.description: Conventional-commit discipline: a skill that writes great messages and a fail-open guard hook that blocks malformed ones.

plugins.name: dep-bump-brief
plugins.description: Turns a dependency-bump diff into a plain-language review brief — old to new, semver distance, real usage sites to check, honest 'changelog not checked' when sources are unreachable.

plugins.name: env-doctor
plugins.description: Diagnoses your dev environment against what the repo actually requires — with copyable fixes and zero unasked mutations.

plugins.name: fork-a-foundry
plugins.description: Bootstrap your own self-running plugin workshop AND the org-pattern framework that governs it — constitution, guard, single-writer orchestrator, the whole company in a box.

plugins.name: night-clerk
plugins.description: The foundry's front desk inside your terminal: ask what plugin helps with a task and get real recommendations with exact install lines from a bundled catalog snapshot.

plugins.name: plugin-smith
plugins.description: Scaffold new Claude Code plugins and health-check existing ones against the official spec.

plugins.name: pr-narrator
plugins.description: Drafts PR titles and descriptions from your branch's actual commits and diff — with honest test notes and risk/rollback.

plugins.name: session-recap
plugins.description: One command ends the session properly: an appended, dated handoff — what changed, decisions, open questions, next steps.

plugins.name: test-gap-nudge
plugins.description: Notices when a session changed source files but no tests, and says so once — a polite Stop-hook nudge, never a block.

plugins.name: todo-ledger
plugins.description: Inventories a repo's TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX comments into a ranked, dated ledger — git-blame age and author per hit, oldest first, with a worst-offenders summary and copyable file:line references.
